citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
Male red poodle #1653 had a moderate amount of tartar and his lower front incisor and an upper right premolar were
loose to the touch. These dental problems were pointed out to the licensee during inspection and hasn't been reported to
the attending veterinarian. Untreated dental problems can cause disomfort and cause additional health problems in dogs.
Ensure that this dog is seen by the attending veterinarian (or another licensed veterinarian) for evaluation and treatment.
***Correct by 2/4/2026.

Dog 1653 did not have his tag; per licensee it has been lost. Dogs must have official identification which can be important
for animal health tracking. Ensure that each dog has an official tag affixed as required.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
Outdoor runs are not getting a daily spot clean to remove excreta. Inadequate daily spot cleaning can cause a build-up of
excreta and increase risk of health problems. Ensure that daily routine of daily spot cleaning is in place to prevent a build-
up of excreta on surfaces that dogs touch.

The last recorded head-to-tail physical exams (wellness checks) by the attending veterinarian were Oct 2023 at the
facility. A call to the AV to verify if updated physical exams have been completed was not returned, however the licensee
thinks the last physical exams were what is in the records. Yearly head-to-tail physical exams by the attending
veterinarian help ensure that each dog is healthy. Ensure to contact the attending veterinarian to get annual physical
exams completed as required.
***Correct by March 2, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
Male red poodle #1653 had a moderate amount of tartar and his lower front incisor and an upper right premolar were 
loose to the touch. These dental problems were pointed out to the licensee during inspection and hasn't been reported to 
the attending veterinarian. Untreated dental problems can cause disomfort and cause additional health problems in dogs. 
Ensure that this dog is seen by the attending veterinarian (or another licensed veterinarian) for evaluation and treatment.  
***Correct by 2/4/2026. 
2.50  Repeat
   
Time and method of identification. 
Dog 1653 did not have his tag; per licensee it has been lost. Dogs must have official identification which can be important 
for animal health tracking. Ensure that each dog has an official tag affixed as required. 
3.11(a)  Repeat
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
Outdoor runs are not getting a daily spot clean to remove excreta. Inadequate daily spot cleaning can cause a build-up of 
excreta and increase risk of health problems. Ensure that daily routine of daily spot cleaning is in place to prevent a build-
up of excreta on surfaces that dogs touch. 
3.13(a)(2) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The last recorded head-to-tail physical exams (wellness checks) by the attending veterinarian were Oct 2023 at the 
facility. A call to the AV to verify if updated physical exams have been completed was not returned, however the licensee 
thinks the last physical exams were what is in the records. Yearly head-to-tail physical exams by the attending 
veterinarian help ensure that each dog is healthy. Ensure to contact the attending veterinarian to get annual physical 
exams completed as required.  
***Correct by March 2, 2026.

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representative.
